title: hyptis microphylla
intro: Hyptis microphylla, also known as Dwarf Hyptis or Pudina, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant is native to Central and South America and is used for its medicinal and aromatic properties. The leaves and flowers of Hyptis microphylla have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or essential oils to harness its medicinal properties and offer health benefits such as relieving cold and flu symptoms, improving digestion, and reducing stress.
